title: शनि पञ्चरत्नम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री शनि पञ्चरत्नम् ॥

नीलाञ्जनसमाभासं सूर्यपुत्रं यमाग्रजम्।
छायामार्तण्डसम्भूतं नमामि शनैश्चरम्॥ १॥

कृष्णवर्णं गदाहस्तं कालदण्डधरं प्रभुम्।
काकवाहनमारूढं प्रणमामि शनीश्वरम्॥ २॥

कर्मफलप्रदातारं न्यायशीलं दयापरम्।
तिलतैलप्रियं देवं प्रपद्ये शनैश्चरम्॥ ३॥

साढेसातिभयच्छेदं कर्मदोषविनाशकम्।
दुःखदारिद्र्यनाशाय शनिमीशं भजाम्यहम्॥ ४॥

कुम्भमीनराशीशं धर्मज्ञं तपसां निधिम्।
भक्तरक्षाकरं शान्तं शनिं प्रणमतां शुभम्॥ ५॥

शनिपञ्चरत्नमिदं यः पठेच्छनिवासरे।
शनिपीडा न बाधेत शनिकृपां लभेद् ध्रुवम्॥

॥ इति श्री शनि पञ्चरत्नम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Shani Pancharatnam ||

Nilanjansamabhasam Suryaputram Yamagrajam |
Chhayamartandasambhutam Namami Shanaishcharam || 1 ||

Krishnavarnam Gadahastam Kaladandadharam Prabhum |
Kakavahanam Arudham Pranamami Shanishvaram || 2 ||

Karmaphalapradataram Nyayashilam Dayaparam |
Tilatailapriyam Devam Prapadye Shanaishcharam || 3 ||

Sadesatibhayachhedam Karmadoshavinashakam |
Duhkhadaridryanashaya Shanimisham Bhajamyaham || 4 ||

KumbhameenaRashisham Dharmajnam Tapasam Nidhim |
Bhaktarakshakaram Shantam Shanim Pranatam Shubham || 5 ||

ShaniPancharatnam Idam Yah Pathechchhanivasare |
Shanipeeda Na Badheta Shanikripam Labhed Dhruvam ||

|| Iti Shri Shani Pancharatnam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
नीलांजन के समान कान्तिवाले, सूर्यपुत्र, यमाग्रज शनैश्चर को नमस्कार।

कृष्णवर्ण, गदाहस्त, कालदण्डधर, काकवाहन शनीश्वर को प्रणाम।

कर्मफलप्रदाता, न्यायशील, दयापर, तिलतैलप्रिय शनि की शरण।

साढ़ेसाती भयच्छेदक, कर्मदोष विनाशक, दुःख-दरिद्र्य नाशक शनि का भजन।

शनिवार को पढ़ने से शनिपीड़ा नहीं बाधती और शनिकृपा निश्चित होती है।
benefits: Five jewel-verses praising Lord Shani's divine qualities
Compact yet powerful remedy for Sade Sati and Shani Dasha
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty, suffering, and karmic afflictions
Invokes Shani as just, compassionate, and protective
Perfect Saturday recitation for Shani graha shanti
description: The Shani Pancharatnam, known in Sanskrit as शनि पञ्चरत्नम्, is five gems of devotional verses in praise of Lord Shani Dev. Rooted in Skanda Purana, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to remove poverty, suffering, and karmic afflictions, compact yet powerful remedy for Sade Sati and Shani Dasha, and invoke Shani as just, compassionate, and protective. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Saturday (Shanivar) and during Shani Jayanti and Shani Amavasya. The text contains 5 verses and takes around 2 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Shani Pancharatnam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Shani Pancharatnam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shani Pancharatnam (शनि पञ्चरत्नम्) is a five-jewel-verse Sanskrit hymn in praise of Lord Shanaishchara — the slow-moving planetary deity (graha) of justice, karma, discipline, and karmic recompense. Preserved in the Skanda Purana stotra tradition, it is one of the most compact yet powerful Shani stotras and is recited daily by devotees experiencing Sade Sati (the seven-and-a-half-year Shani transit), Dhaiya (the two-and-a-half-year Shani transit), or Shani Mahadasha. The five verses successively invoke Shani as: (1) the dark-collyrium-coloured son of Surya and elder brother of Yama, born of Chhaya (verse 1); (2) the black-bodied gada-bearing crow-vehicled lord (verse 2); (3) the giver of karma-fruits, just and compassionate, fond of sesame oil (verse 3); (4) the destroyer of Sade Sati fear and karma-doshas, dispeller of suffering and poverty (verse 4); (5) the lord of Aquarius and Pisces, knower of dharma, treasury of tapas, protector of devotees (verse 5). (Sources: Skanda Purana stotra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Shani Pancharatnam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shani Pancharatnam is most powerful when recited on Saturday (Shanivar — Shani's principal day) — ideally at dusk (sandhya-kala) facing west, after lighting a chaumukhi sesame-oil deepam under a Pipal (peepal) tree or before a Shani idol. The most auspicious days of the year are: Shani Jayanti (Vaishakha Krishna Amavasya / Jyeshtha Amavasya — Shani Dev's appearance day), Shani Amavasya (any Saturday falling on Amavasya), Shani Pradosh (any Saturday Pradosh), Shani Trayodashi, and during Sade Sati / Dhaiya / Shani Mahadasha periods. Devotees offer black sesame seeds (kala til), sesame oil, urad dal, black cloth, and iron items; feed crows (Shani's vahana) and the poor; and conclude with the prayer Om Sham Shanaishcharaya Namah. The phalashruti specifically promises that Shani-peeda (Shani affliction) will not afflict the daily reciter and Shani-kripa is assured. (Sources: Skanda Purana; traditional Shani upasana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: Is the Shani Pancharatnam effective for Sade Sati and Shani Mahadasha?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes — the Shani Pancharatnam is one of the classical remedies (parihara) prescribed for Sade Sati and Shani Mahadasha. Verse 4 explicitly addresses this: Sade-sati-bhaya-chchedam karma-dosha-vinashakam, duhkha-daridrya-nashaya Shanim-isham bhajamy-aham — I worship Lord Shani as the dispeller of Sade Sati fear, destroyer of karma-doshas, and remover of suffering and poverty. Sade Sati is the 7.5-year period when Shani transits the sign before, during, and after one's natal Moon sign, traditionally feared for its karmic weight; Dhaiya is the parallel 2.5-year transit through 4th and 8th houses from Moon. Shani Mahadasha is the 19-year planetary period in Vimshottari Dasha. The Pancharatnam — combined with Shani Chalisa, Shani Stotra, Hanuman Chalisa (Hanuman is Shani's protector), and dana of black sesame, urad and oil — is the standard parihara prescribed by Vaidic Jyotishis. (Sources: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra; Phaladeepika Shani parihara tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Shani called Suryaputra and Yamagraja?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 1 of the Shani Pancharatnam invokes Shani as Suryaputram Yamagrajam — the son of Surya and elder brother of Yama. Mythologically, Shani's mother is Chhaya (Shadow) — the chaya-pratima (shadow-image) of Surya's principal consort Sanjna, who was unable to bear Surya's intense brilliance and replaced herself temporarily with Chhaya. From Chhaya, Surya begot Shani, Yama (Dharmaraja, lord of death), Yamuna (the river goddess), Tapti (another river), and Manu Vaivasvata (progenitor of the present Manu-antara). Shani is depicted as the elder brother (agraja) of Yama, both being judges of dharma — Yama judges karma after death, Shani judges karma during life through karma-phala-vipaka (the timing and manner of karmic fruition). Thus Shani's role is karma-data (giver of karma-fruits) — strict but not cruel, just but compassionate. (Sources: Skanda Purana; Brahma Vaivarta Purana; Markandeya Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Shani Pancharatnam, Shani Stotra and Shani Ashtottara?
seoFaqs.answer: All three are Shani prayers but differ in length, focus and ritual purpose. The Shani Pancharatnam is a compact 5-verse hymn from Skanda Purana — quick to recite (under 2 minutes), focuses on Shani's iconography and explicit Sade Sati relief — ideal for daily recitation. The Shani Stotra (the famous Dashanama Stotra by Dasaratha) is a 10-name composition by King Dasharatha himself when he successfully averted Sade Sati from his kingdom — recited primarily for Sade Sati relief and considered the most powerful Shani prayer. The Shani Ashtottara Shatanamavali is a 108-name litany used for archana (offering one tila/sesame seed or black urad dal per name), recited during formal Shani puja and graha-shanti homas. The Pancharatnam is for daily compact stuti, the Dashanama for Sade Sati emergency, the Ashtottara for formal archana. (Sources: Skanda Purana; Padma Purana Dasharatha-krita Shani Stotra.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does daily recitation of Shani Pancharatnam grant?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti declares: Shani-pancharatnam-idam yah pathech-Shanivasare, Shani-peeda na badheta Shani-kripam labhed dhruvam — the one who recites this Shani Pancharatnam on Saturday is not afflicted by Shani's pain and assuredly attains Shani's grace. Tradition further attributes the following: (1) Sade Sati / Dhaiya / Shani Mahadasha relief — softening of Shani's karmic strikes, (2) karma-dosha-nasha — destruction of accumulated karmic blemishes, (3) duhkha-daridrya-nasha — removal of suffering and poverty, (4) employment, court case, property and legal-matter resolution (Shani's domains), (5) longevity and health — Shani is the karaka of Ayur-bhava (longevity house in astrology), (6) discipline, perseverance and tapas, (7) Hanuman's grace by extension (Hanuman-Shani friendship story), (8) iron-clad protection from Shani's negative phases, (9) the promised Shani-kripa (compassion of the strict but just judge of karma). (Sources: Shani Pancharatnam phalashruti; Skanda Purana.)
